title: Fix Table 3 CI inversion
description: In 5 of 7 rows, the 95% CI lower bound exceeds the point estimate (e.g., 36.1% with CI [37.9%, 42.1%]). This is mathematically impossible and will be flagged by any reviewer within minutes.
action: Recompute all bootstrap CIs using per-token aggregation (matching the point estimates). If per-word CIs are desired, present matched per-word point estimates. Never publish CIs that exclude their own point estimate.

title: Verify cross-domain patching data provenance
description: The sign reversal from d=-0.91 (FAILED pilot) to d=+1.50 (corrected FULL) for city-continent activation patching has not been independently verified. The phase0_data_integrity task was 'skipped for speed'. The paper's 'universal mechanism' claim (Contribution 2) rests entirely on this unverified correction.
action: Run a 20-entity spot-check: randomly sample 20 city-continent entities, re-run full activation patching pipeline, verify recovery rate is consistent with 61.9%. Document the spot-check results and the original bug fix in an appendix note.

title: Run city-continent probe degradation
description: The probe degradation curve extrapolates from synthetic Gaussian noise on first-letter binary probes to predict RAVEL multi-class probe effects. This extrapolation is untested. The skeptic (result debate) identified this as the paper's methodological Achilles' heel.
action: Degrade city-continent probes (F1=0.87 baseline) to 3-4 lower F1 levels via same noise injection protocol. Measure absorption at each level. If the slope matches first-letter curve (within 2x), the extrapolation is validated. If it differs, reframe Section 4.6 claims.

title: Reconcile aggregation-dependent absorption rates
description: Three different first-letter L24 16k absorption rates: 27.1% (per-word, paper canonical), 21.6% (per-token, probe degradation control), 34.5% (per-unique-word, earlier iterations). The 21.6% control falls below the 27.1% CI [26.3%, 34.7%]. Readers comparing Table 2 (27.1%) with Table 3 (21.6%) will find contradictory numbers.
action: Choose per-token as canonical aggregation. Recompute ALL absorption rates (Tables 2, 3, 4) using per-token. Add a footnote explaining the aggregation choice and sensitivity analysis. If per-word rates are retained for backward compatibility, present both with explicit labels.

title: Implement validate_integration.py
description: Automated claim-data cross-check has been recommended for 10 consecutive iterations and never implemented. 5 of 18 findings in this review are data integrity failures that it would have caught.
action: Create validate_integration.py that: (1) reads all numerical claims from paper.md via regex, (2) checks each against a manifest JSON mapping claims to source data files, (3) reports mismatches with file:line references. Run before every paper revision.

title: Reorder contributions to lead with probe degradation
description: The paper leads with 'cross-domain absorption characterization' but the probe degradation ablation (R^2=0.777) undermines this claim for 2 of 3 RAVEL hierarchies. The real primary contribution is the probe degradation methodology. Result debate verdict (6.5) recommended this restructuring.
action: Restructure abstract and introduction: (1) Probe degradation methodology (the methodological advance, R^2=0.777), (2) Universal causal mechanism (the empirical advance, d=0.75-1.50), (3) Cross-domain characterization with confound decomposition (the application), (4) Quadruple correlational failure (the negative boundary).

title: Replace 4.1x headline with accurate figure
description: 45.1/11.6 = 3.89x (not 4.1x). The 4.1x used the stale city-continent value of 42.9%. Additionally, the quality-gated range (excluding city-country F1=0.73) is 2.7x (31.4/11.6). The 4.1x appears in 5+ locations.
action: Replace all instances of '4.1x' with '3.9x' (full range) or '2.7x (quality-gated)'. Present city-country as supplementary/exploratory throughout. Clearly distinguish descriptive range from quality-gated range.

title: Fix layer multiplier inconsistency
description: Paper says 26x (Section 4.2), 15x (Section 7.4, outline), and the data supports 11x (if L6=2.4%). Three different multipliers for the same phenomenon.
action: Determine correct L6 first-letter absorption rate from source data. Compute L24/L6 ratio. Use ONE consistent figure throughout. If the '15x' refers to a cross-hierarchy average, state this explicitly.

title: Eliminate Section 7.2 verbatim repetition
description: Section 7.2 restates Section 5.1-5.2 recovery rates, effect sizes, p-values, and interpretation near-verbatim. The activation patching results appear with full numerical detail in 4 locations (5.1, 5.2, 7.2, 8).
action: Section 5: keep full detail. Section 7.2: discuss IMPLICATIONS only (deployment, theory constraints, future experiments), reference Section 5 for numbers. Section 8: one-sentence summary with range only.

title: Explain city-country patching exclusion
description: City-country (45.1% absorption, highest) is excluded from activation patching without any explanation. The paper claims 'universal' mechanism but omits the highest-absorption hierarchy from causal testing.
action: Either (a) run city-country patching (0.5 GPU-hours) or (b) add an explicit paragraph in Section 5.2 and Section 7.6 explaining the exclusion (probe quality F1=0.73 below gate, patching results unreliable).

title: Fix Figure 4 layer mismatch
description: Panel 1 (first-letter) shows L12, Panels 2-3 (city-continent, city-language) show L24. Comparison across panels uses different layers.
action: Preferred: re-run first-letter patching at L24 (or cite existing L24 data if available). Alternative: add caption note explaining the layer difference and why it does not invalidate the comparison.

title: Fix Figure 5 caption-content mismatch and N discrepancy
description: Caption promises overlay of first-letter (N=158) and city-continent (N=1,464) distributions. Figure appears to show only city-continent. Legend says N=1471, text says N=1,464.
action: Re-render with both distributions (different colors or side-by-side panels). Reconcile N=1471 vs N=1464 (pre-cleaning vs post-cleaning?) and use the correct number consistently.

title: Reconcile Figure 6 p-value with text
description: Figure 6 shows 'Hier: p=0.063' at L24 but Section 6 says hierarchy p=0.041.
action: Check if the figure and text use different statistical tests. Reconcile or explain the difference in the caption.

title: Reduce abstract information density
description: The abstract contains 15+ distinct quantitative results in ~290 words. Too dense for a first read.
action: Reduce to 5-7 key numbers: the headline range, the best probe degradation statistic (R^2=0.777), the best patching effect size (d=0.75-1.50), and one summary of correlational failure. Move the rest to the introduction.

title: Fix hype words
description: Section 2.1: 'dominant tool' (unsupported superlative). Section 7.4: 'amplify this concern' (hype transition).
action: 'dominant' -> 'widely adopted'. 'amplify' -> 'are relevant to' or lead directly with the aspects.

title: Update proposal with current data
description: Proposal reports city-continent as 42.9% (paper says 31.4%), uses 4x range (should be 3.9x), 15x variation (inconsistent with paper).
action: Update proposal.md to match paper.md for all numerical claims. While the proposal is a planning document, workspace consistency prevents confusion for the reflection agent.
